title: Logistics Coordinator

rationales.taskAutomation: Shipment tracking, route optimization, and standard carrier comparison are now largely automated, but multi-modal exception handling and customs judgment calls keep over a third of the role human-driven.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI logistics and visibility platforms are credible, fast-improving, and increasingly the default tooling on top of transportation-management systems.
rationales.adoption: Common among tech-forward retail, manufacturing, and 3PL employers; spreading but not yet universal among smaller shippers.
rationales.agenticExposure: Routine tracking-and-routing chains are agent-ownable today; live-fire disruption response and carrier relationship repair still need a person.

tasksAtRisk: Shipment tracking and automated status updates
Route and carrier optimization for standard lanes
Rate comparison and carrier selection for routine loads
tasksGrowing: Exception handling during disruptions and delays
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1081 (Logisticians); range reflects coordinator-level positions within the broader logistician occupation
insight: Tracking, routing, and standard rate comparison are now largely automated by TMS copilots, but real supply chains still break in ways only a person can untangle. Coordinators who specialize in disruption response and carrier relationships are more durable than those doing pure status-update work.
